items.markdown:
**Alert fatigue** is `the mental desensitization and operational burnout that occurs when workers are overwhelmed by an excessive number of notifications` —many of which are low-priority or false positives. It is the modern equivalent of "crying wolf": repeated false warnings cause people to subconsciously tune out alerts, leading to delayed responses or missed critical events.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue)[[2]](https://www.pagerduty.com/resources/digital-operations/learn/alert-fatigue/)[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alarm_fatigue)[[4]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CN_sRMtMPmk&t=52)

The phenomenon is widespread across several high-stakes industries:

- **Healthcare:** Nurses and doctors are bombarded by thousands of daily equipment or medication alerts, which can result in missed warnings about a patient's deteriorating condition.[](https://www.pharmaceuticalpress.com/insights/article/what-is-alert-fatigue-in-healthcare/) [[1]](https://www.pharmaceuticalpress.com/insights/article/what-is-alert-fatigue-in-healthcare/)[[2]](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK555522/)
- **Cybersecurity:** Security analysts receive millions of threat alerts daily, often leading them to ignore or dismiss notifications without proper investigation.[](https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/cyberpedia/how-to-reduce-security-alert-fatigue) [[1]](https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/cyberpedia/how-to-reduce-security-alert-fatigue)[[2]](https://www.deepwatch.com/education-center/what-is-alert-fatigue/)
- **IT and Software (DevOps):** On-call engineers receive an unending stream of server notifications, leading to system downtime, missed critical outages, and severe employee burnout.[](https://www.xurrent.com/blog/reduce-alert-fatigue) [[1]](https://www.xurrent.com/blog/reduce-alert-fatigue)[[2]](https://www.proofpoint.com/uk/threat-reference/alert-fatigue)

**Common Causes** 

- **Poor Signal-to-Noise Ratio:** Systems are configured too sensitively, triggering alarms for minor, non-actionable events.
- **False Positives:** The system flags an issue that isn't actually a problem or a threat.
- **Volume Overload:** There is simply too much data for a human to process in real-time.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue)[[2]](https://www.pharmaceuticalpress.com/insights/article/what-is-alert-fatigue-in-healthcare/)[[3]](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK555522/)[[4]](https://www.deepwatch.com/education-center/what-is-alert-fatigue/)[[5]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gqTxyvI52v8&t=4)[[6]](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12181921/)

**Mitigation Strategies**   
Organizations combat alert fatigue by refining their monitoring systems:[](https://www.atlassian.com/incident-management/on-call/alert-fatigue) [[1]](https://www.atlassian.com/incident-management/on-call/alert-fatigue)[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alarm_fatigue)

- **Tuning and Filtering:** Adjusting thresholds so only actionable, high-priority issues trigger an alert.
- **Intelligent Routing:** Ensuring that different types of alerts are escalated only to the personnel specifically equipped to handle them.
- **Contextual Automation:** Integrating AI or automated scripts to investigate or resolve low-level warnings before a human ever needs to be notified.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue)[[2]](https://www.proofpoint.com/uk/threat-reference/alert-fatigue)[[3]](https://www.atlassian.com/incident-management/on-call/alert-fatigue)[[4]](https://www.pagerduty.com/resources/digital-operations/learn/alert-fatigue/)[[5]](https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/cyberpedia/how-to-reduce-security-alert-fatigue)

To read more about the psychological and operational impacts, explore the [IBM Alert Fatigue Guide](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/alert-fatigue) or the [PagerDuty Resource Center](https://www.pagerduty.com/resources/digital-operations/learn/alert-fatigue/) for ways modern teams are optimizing their incident management.[[1]](https://www.pagerduty.com/resources/digital-operations/learn/alert-fatigue/)

expanded_element.title: What Is Alert Fatigue? Causes, Risks & How to Reduce It | Panther
expanded_element.description: More specifically, alert fatigue is generally caused by: High false positive rates from security tools : Many security teams face high false positive rates of up to 99%, with analysts spending significant time chasing alerts that turn out to be nothing.

expanded_element.title: What Is Alert Fatigue? | IBM
expanded_element.description: Alert fatigue is a state of mental and operational exhaustion caused by an overwhelming number of alerts —many of which are low priority, false positives or otherwise non-actionable.

items.title: What is alert fatigue in cyber security?

expanded_element.title: Alert Fatigue: How to Fix SOC Overload - Cymulate
expanded_element.description: Alert fatigue refers to a state where cybersecurity analysts become overwhelmed by the sheer volume of alerts, making it difficult to distinguish real threats from harmless noise. Though these alerts are intended to enhance visibility, they often create an environment where meaningful signals are lost in the noise.

items.title: What is alert fatigue in a hospital setting?

expanded_element.title: What is alert fatigue in healthcare? - Pharmaceutical Press
expanded_element.description: A key persistent issue with clinical decision support (CDS) systems is alert fatigue, where clinically important alerts are ignored alongside alerts that are not clinically important or relevant. If clinicians are presented with too many warnings, they may become desensitised and start to ignore them.

items.title: How do you avoid alert fatigue?
What are the top 3 causes of fatigue?

expanded_element.title: Fatigue as the Chief Complaint: Epidemiology, Causes ... - PMC - NIH
expanded_element.description: Sleep disorders, sleep-related respiratory disorders, depression, and psychosocial stress are the main causes of persistent fatigue.
